# Refine README.md presentation: apex, scale, maturity, headline

## Context

Follow-up to the identity-apex rollout (Epic #12225; the README apex landed via #12228 / PR #12243). With the apex *content* now in `README.md`, the operator flagged (2026-05-31) that the README's **presentation** isn't there yet — four concrete issues. Grounded in current `dev` `README.md`.

## The Problems (operator-flagged)

1. **Apex not fully featured (opening, lines ~13–31).** The opening condenses/paraphrases the apex. The graduated canonical hero (Discussion #12234, operator-authored) is richer — it includes the possession-interface detail ("inhabits live applications, inspecting semantic runtime state, mutating UI and data in real time, turning conversational UIs from chat panels into agents collaborating inside the application"), DreamService re-steering, self-healing loops, and the close: *"In May 2026, the canonical repo recorded 706 merged PRs and 800 closed issues. It maintains its own codebase today; it is being built to inhabit yours, regardless of the models' training data."* The README should feature that full graduated apex — **but broken into readable structure, not one wall of text.** Operator: *"of course should not be one wall of text, but the readme is not there yet."*
2. **"A Platform at Scale (State of May 1, 2026)" (line 164) — too much number-bragging.** A ~10-line scale section is fine; the current section is an exhaustive line-by-line breakdown (8 engine line-items + subtotal + embedded-knowledge + more) that reads as excessive bragging → negative. **Trim to ~10 lines** (keep a representative scale signal, cut the line-by-line).
3. **"Faculty-Staging Maturity (Honest Current State)" (line 92) — vastly outdated.** The faculty table (Speech/Attention shipped; Short-term-recall #10332, Dream #10030 "in flight") + the SPOF / resilience-asymmetry framing (#10291) are stale. **Refresh to current state** (or reconsider the section); V-B-A the faculty statuses + #10332 / #10030 / #10291 first.
4. **"This is the headline." (line 22, Brain bullet) — confusing.** Reads as meaningless to a cold reader (operator: *"i don't get what that is supposed to mean"*). **Remove/rephrase** — and reconsider the parallel *"This is the adoption substrate the Brain inhabits and improves"* on the Body bullet (line 23).
5. **MX-loop + motto block — KEEP (it's strong); reframe ONLY the gated-RSI clause (operator, 2026-05-31).** The opening's MX-loop block must be **preserved**:
   > *Neo's evolution mechanism is the **MX loop** — Model Experience as production mechanism. Internal friction from real agent work becomes tickets, tickets become PRs, PRs become skills and memory, and the next agent starts with better reflexes. The trajectory is **autonomous narrow intelligence (ANI)** by accumulation, [gated-RSI clause — see below].*
   >
   > *"The system evolves by predicting its own evolution."*

   **Preserve this block — and ESPECIALLY the motto quote.** *"The system evolves by predicting its own evolution"* is the canonical recurring motto; its origin is [`learn/agentos/DreamPipeline.md`](./learn/agentos/DreamPipeline.md) (per the `neo-identity-update` facts-ledger recurring-motto SSOT — line 32/354 there). Do not drop or paraphrase it.

   **The ONE change:** the gated-RSI clause — currently *"under gated-RSI: agents propose, humans approve at merge"* — must be reframed. Per Discussion #12234 `dc-17119184` + ADR 0018 §2.7 OD-1 governance-line, gated-RSI is a founder-architect **governance choice** (the swarm **can** run the lifecycle autonomously; the founder-architect keeps final merge authority as an intentional governance choice — product taste, strategic coherence, accountable ownership), **NOT** a generic "humans approve" HITL-safety gate or a capability limit. Suggested form (tight, matching the block's voice): *"…under gated-RSI by design: the swarm runs the lifecycle; the founder-architect keeps final merge authority as a governance choice."* Same correction that landed in VISION §5 (PR #12246, cycle-4).

## The Architectural Reality
- `README.md` is the highest-visibility identity surface; it should lead with the canonical apex (ADR 0018 §2.7 **OD-1**) and carry the two-hemispheres framing (**OD-3**) — the opening already does the two-hemispheres structure; **keep that**.
- The full graduated apex hero lives in **Discussion #12234** (operator-authored README/portal-hero form) + ADR 0018 §2.7 OD-1.

## The Fix
1. Feature the full graduated #12234 apex hero in the opening, broken into readable paragraphs (not a wall of text); preserve the two-hemispheres structure.
2. Trim "Platform at Scale" to a concise ~10-line scale section.
3. Refresh / reconsider "Faculty-Staging Maturity" against verified current state.
4. Remove/rephrase "This is the headline." (+ the parallel Body-bullet phrasing).
5. Preserve the MX-loop + motto block (incl. the `DreamPipeline.md`-origin motto verbatim); reframe ONLY the gated-RSI clause to the governance-choice form (per #12234 `dc-17119184`), consistent with VISION §5.

**Ownership (operator-directed):** implementation PR authored by @neo-opus-4-7; cross-family review by @neo-gpt. (I drive the lifecycle — author the PR, then request review.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README opening features the full graduated #12234 apex (possession interface, self-healing loops, "706 PRs / inhabit yours" close), broken into readable structure — not a wall of text.
- [ ] "Platform at Scale" trimmed to ~10 lines; no exhaustive line-by-line number breakdown.
- [ ] "Faculty-Staging Maturity" refreshed to verified current state (or reconsidered); #10332 / #10030 / #10291 states re-checked.
- [ ] "This is the headline" removed/rephrased; Brain/Body bullet phrasing reads cleanly cold.
- [ ] MX-loop + motto block preserved; the canonical motto *"The system evolves by predicting its own evolution"* (DreamPipeline.md origin) retained verbatim.
- [ ] gated-RSI clause reframed to founder-architect governance-choice (per #12234 `dc-17119184` / OD-1 governance-line) — not "humans approve at merge" / capability-limit; consistent with VISION §5.
- [ ] Cross-family review (identity surface) + operator wording-eyes before merge.

## Out of Scope
- `.github/VISION.md` (#12229 / PR #12246 — separate sub).
- The two-hemispheres structure itself (keep — OD-3-aligned).
- The MX-loop + motto block content (KEEP — only the gated-RSI clause within it changes).
- Other already-merged identity surfaces.

## Decision Record impact
`aligned-with` ADR 0018 §2.7 OD-1 (apex) + OD-3 (two hemispheres). No ADR change.

## Related
- Parent epic: #12225 (identity rollout)
- Predecessor: #12228 / PR #12243 (README apex — merged; this refines its presentation)
- Canonical apex source: Discussion #12234 (operator-authored hero) + ADR 0018 §2.7 OD-1
- Sibling open: #12229 / PR #12246 (VISION)

- 2026-05-31T12:23:02Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `9c2a2da` - "docs(identity): drop framework comparisons + Neo.mjs branding consistency (#12251)

- Remove the category-drift anti-pattern 'makes Neo an organism rather than a framework' (§neo_identity_anchor: never define Neo.mjs by contrast to 'framework' — it concedes the category it transcends). Describe the Brain on its own terms (the self-evolving engineering institution).

- Drop the two other framework/competitor comparisons: 'not a framework-shopping option' and the 'React with a different syntax' / Astro/Next.js audience-filter (kept the audience-exclusion, removed the framework names).

- Brand-subject 'Neo' -> 'Neo.mjs' consistently in the prominent prose (hero, section intros, closer, Contributing) for branding + SEO disambiguation (operator preference; bare 'Neo' is ambiguous to crawlers)."
- 2026-05-31T12:36:55Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `bbb1b30` - "docs(identity): replace stale faculty-staging maturity table with verified SPOF caveat (#12251)

The 'Faculty-Staging Maturity' table (Speech/Attention shipped; Short-term-recall/Dream in flight) reflected the v12.1.0 release era (~1300 merged PRs ago) and contradicted the apex ('autonomously runs the full engineering lifecycle … 706 merged PRs'). My earlier count tweak (three->two faculties) matched the stale table instead of verifying current state — a V-B-A failure.

V-B-A: package version 12.1.0 (2026-03-27) but dev is ~1300 PRs ahead and operationally autonomous; the only verified-current honest caveat is the resilience asymmetry — the Brain is centralized (SPOF, #10291 still OPEN). Dropped the stale table; kept the apex-consistent + verified SPOF caveat; renamed the section."
- 2026-05-31T12:45:53Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `ce856e6` - "docs(identity): feature v13 multi-tenant cloud Agent OS — deployable on your codebase (#12251)

The README framed 'inhabit yours' as future-aspirational; v13 is the multi-tenant cloud Agent OS in active deployment. Adds a prominent section: the Agent OS ships as the canonical cloud topology (ADR 0014 + ai/deploy) — KB + Memory Core + Native Edge Graph + cloud-safe Orchestrator + OIDC ingress — that ingests + reasons over external tenant repos with per-tenant identity/visibility isolation ('one Brain, many tenants; a config entry, not a fork').

Capability framing only (no client/partner naming); ties to the apex's 'being built to inhabit yours' second clause. Accurate to active-deployment (not GA/battle-tested)."
- 2026-05-31T13:29:09Z @tobiu referenced in commit `9debe7c` - "docs(identity): refine README presentation — feature apex, trim scale, fix gated-RSI/headline (#12251) (#12252)

* docs(identity): refine README presentation — feature apex, trim scale, fix gated-RSI/headline (#12251)

- Opening now features the full graduated apex (Discussion #12234 hero), broken into readable paragraphs (possession interface, self-healing loops, the 706-PRs / 'inhabit yours' close) instead of a condensed paraphrase.

- Reframe gated-RSI as a founder-architect governance choice (not 'agents propose, humans approve at merge') across the opening MX block, the Brain section, and Contributing — per Discussion #12234 dc-17119184 / ADR 0018 OD-1.

- Fix the confusing 'This is the headline' / 'adoption substrate' Brain/Body bullet phrasings.

- Trim 'A Platform at Scale' from a ~50-line per-directory breakdown to a ~10-line summary + Codebase Overview link.

- Preserve the MX-loop block + canonical motto verbatim (DreamPipeline.md origin).

- Maturity section: corrected the prose/table count inconsistency; the deeper 'vastly outdated' refresh is flagged for operator current-state input (not guessed).

* docs(identity): drop framework comparisons + Neo.mjs branding consistency (#12251)

- Remove the category-drift anti-pattern 'makes Neo an organism rather than a framework' (§neo_identity_anchor: never define Neo.mjs by contrast to 'framework' — it concedes the category it transcends). Describe the Brain on its own terms (the self-evolving engineering institution).

- Drop the two other framework/competitor comparisons: 'not a framework-shopping option' and the 'React with a different syntax' / Astro/Next.js audience-filter (kept the audience-exclusion, removed the framework names).

- Brand-subject 'Neo' -> 'Neo.mjs' consistently in the prominent prose (hero, section intros, closer, Contributing) for branding + SEO disambiguation (operator preference; bare 'Neo' is ambiguous to crawlers).

* docs(identity): replace stale faculty-staging maturity table with verified SPOF caveat (#12251)

The 'Faculty-Staging Maturity' table (Speech/Attention shipped; Short-term-recall/Dream in flight) reflected the v12.1.0 release era (~1300 merged PRs ago) and contradicted the apex ('autonomously runs the full engineering lifecycle … 706 merged PRs'). My earlier count tweak (three->two faculties) matched the stale table instead of verifying current state — a V-B-A failure.

V-B-A: package version 12.1.0 (2026-03-27) but dev is ~1300 PRs ahead and operationally autonomous; the only verified-current honest caveat is the resilience asymmetry — the Brain is centralized (SPOF, #10291 still OPEN). Dropped the stale table; kept the apex-consistent + verified SPOF caveat; renamed the section.

* docs(identity): feature v13 multi-tenant cloud Agent OS — deployable on your codebase (#12251)

The README framed 'inhabit yours' as future-aspirational; v13 is the multi-tenant cloud Agent OS in active deployment. Adds a prominent section: the Agent OS ships as the canonical cloud topology (ADR 0014 + ai/deploy) — KB + Memory Core + Native Edge Graph + cloud-safe Orchestrator + OIDC ingress — that ingests + reasons over external tenant repos with per-tenant identity/visibility isolation ('one Brain, many tenants; a config entry, not a fork').

Capability framing only (no client/partner naming); ties to the apex's 'being built to inhabit yours' second clause. Accurate to active-deployment (not GA/battle-tested).
